message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My feature (process_priority for click.edit) requires editing both _termui_impl.py (Editor class) and termui.py (edit function signature). What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My feature: multi-file support for click.edit(). Need to edit Editor.edit_file->edit_files in _termui_impl.py and edit() signature in termui.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both our features (multi-file edit vs process_priority) necessarily touch the same two files (Editor class + edit() signature) - true disjoint file ownership isn't possible here. Since we each work in our own isolated repo copy and submit independent patch.txt files, I propose we both keep these same files but focus edits narrowly: I will only touch the 'filename' param (support str|Iterable[str]) and edit_file->edit_files rename/logic, leaving other params/signature untouched aside from adding my new param plumbing. Please keep your process_priority changes additive (new param) so a future merge would just combine both diffs with minimal conflict. Proceeding on this basis unless you object.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Full file overlap is unavoidable here - both features touch Editor.__init__/edit_file and termui.edit(). Propose logical split instead of file split: I will ONLY add the new process_priority parameter (appended at the END of each signature, after existing params like extension) plus a self-contained priority-setting helper/method - I won't touch filename/edit_file(s) logic. You own edit_file->edit_files renaming and multi-file handling - please don't touch process_priority code. Suggest param order: keep your filenames changes first, I'll append process_priority as the last param in both signatures so our diffs don't collide on the same line. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed: I own filename/edit_file->edit_files renaming and multi-file handling only, won't touch process_priority. You append process_priority as last param in both signatures with your own helper. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I will add process_priority as a new, purely additive parameter appended at the end of Editor.__init__ and edit() signatures, with self-contained priority-setting logic that doesn't touch filename/edit_file(s) code. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementation complete: added process_priority (None/low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high) as the last param in Editor.__init__ and edit(), a new _set_process_priority() helper (Windows via ctypes SetPriorityClass, Unix via os.setpriority) with silent best-effort failure handling, and a single insertion point in Editor.edit_file right after subprocess.Popen creation. Did not touch filename/edit_file(s) logic. Tests pass. Wrote diff to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementation complete: renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), joins each filename quoted and space-separated for the subprocess command. termui.edit()'s filename param now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None; normalizes to a tuple and calls edit_files(). Added CHANGES.rst entry. Tests in tests/test_termui.py pass (62 passed). Wrote diff to patch.txt.
